questions.question: Explain the differences between IPv4 and IPv6 header fields.
keyPoints: IPv4 header is variable (20-60 bytes), IPv6 fixed at 40 bytes
IPv6 removed: Header Length, Identification, Flags, Fragment Offset, Options
IPv6 added: Flow Label, Traffic Class (replaces ToS), Next Header (replaces Protocol)
TTL renamed to Hop Limit in IPv6
IPv4 source/destination: 32 bits each, IPv6: 128 bits each

questions.question: What are IPv6 Extension Headers? List any four types with their purposes.
keyPoints: Hop-by-Hop Options: Carries options for all routers
Routing Header: Specifies packet path (like loose source routing)
Fragment Header: Handles source fragmentation
Destination Options: Options for destination node
Authentication Header (AH): Provides data integrity and authentication
ESP: Provides encryption and confidentiality

questions.question: Explain the concept of Path MTU Discovery in IPv6.
keyPoints: Mechanism to find minimum MTU along the path
Source sends packet with size = path MTU
If packet too large, router sends ICMPv6 'Packet Too Big' message
Source reduces packet size and retries
Process continues until packet reaches destination
IPv6 routers do not fragment, so this is mandatory

questions.question: What are the three main IPv6 address types? Explain each briefly.
keyPoints: Unicast: Identifies single interface - packet delivered to that specific interface
Multicast: Identifies group of interfaces - packet delivered to all members of the group
Anycast: Identifies group of interfaces - packet delivered to nearest (in routing terms) interface in the group
Note: IPv6 has no broadcast address - replaced by multicast

questions.question: Explain how Stateless Address Autoconfiguration (SLAAC) works in IPv6.
keyPoints: Host receives network prefix from Router Advertisement (RA)
Host generates interface identifier using EUI-64 (from MAC) or random
Host combines prefix + interface identifier to form 64-bit IPv6 address
Host performs Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) to verify uniqueness
No DHCPv6 server required for basic configuration

questions.question: Describe the IPv6 addressing scheme for Global Unicast Addresses.
keyPoints: Format: n bits (global routing prefix) + m bits (subnet ID) + 64 bits (interface identifier)
Global routing prefix: First 48 bits (typically 2000::/3)
Subnet ID: Next 16 bits for subnetting
Interface ID: Last 64 bits (commonly generated via EUI-64 or random)
2000::/3 is currently the allocated global unicast range

questions.question: What is the purpose of ICMPv6? List any three ICMPv6 message types.
keyPoints: Error reporting and diagnostics
Used in Neighbor Discovery protocol
Used in Path MTU Discovery
Message types: Destination Unreachable, Packet Too Big, Time Exceeded, Echo Request/Reply
Also handles Router/Router Solicitation and Router/Neighbor Advertisement

questions.question: Draw and explain the IPv6 datagram header format with all fields and their sizes.
keyPoints: Diagram: Version(4), Traffic Class(8), Flow Label(20) = 32 bits first row
Second row: Payload Length(16), Next Header(8), Hop Limit(8)
Third-Fourth rows: Source Address (128 bits)
Fifth-Sixth rows: Destination Address (128 bits)
Total: 320 bits = 40 bytes
Explanation of each field purpose
Contrast with IPv4 header differences

questions.question: Explain the transition mechanisms from IPv4 to IPv6.
keyPoints: Dual Stack: Both IPv4 and IPv6 running on same device
Tunneling: IPv6 packets encapsulated in IPv4 packets for transport over IPv4 networks
Types: Manual tunnels, 6to4, Teredo, ISATAP
Translation: IPv6-to-IPv4 translation at network boundary
NAT64/DNS64: Allows IPv6-only clients to communicate with IPv4 servers
Dual Stack is most recommended, tunneling for isolated networks, translation when needed

questions.question: Compare IPv4 and IPv6 with respect to: (i) Address space, (ii) Header size, (iii) Fragmentation, (iv) Security, (v) Address configuration.
keyPoints: (i) Address space: IPv4=32-bit, IPv6=128-bit
(ii) Header size: IPv4=20-60 bytes (variable), IPv6=40 bytes (fixed)
(iii) Fragmentation: IPv4=routers+source, IPv6=source only with PMTUD
(iv) Security: IPv4=optional IPSec, IPv6=built-in IPSec support (AH, ESP)
(v) Address config: IPv4=DHCP/manual, IPv6=SLAAC/DHCPv6/manual
Additional points: QoS (Flow Label), No broadcast in IPv6, etc.

questions.question: Explain IPv6 Neighbor Discovery Protocol and its functions.
keyPoints: ICMPv6-based protocol for autoconfiguration
Functions: Router Discovery, Prefix Discovery, Parameter Discovery
Address Resolution: Maps IPv6 to MAC addresses (replaces ARP)
Duplicate Address Detection (DAD): Verifies address uniqueness
Neighbor Unreachability Detection (NUD): Checks if neighbor is reachable
Router Solicitation (RS) and Router Advertisement (RA) messages
Neighbor Solicitation (NS) and Neighbor Advertisement (NA) messages
